beats.activity: Wake up, shower, breakfast at home
beats.reasoning: Tyler is a workaholic SDE3 who targets 7:30am arrival; he wakes early to prepare. Full energy on day one.
Short commute from South Lake Union to Amazon HQ; arrives by 7:35am as his routine dictates.

beats.activity: Deep work on SDE3 projects, code review, architecture discussions
beats.reasoning: Typical intense morning sprint; Tyler dives into technical work immediately. The 11am FIFA match (USA vs Wales) is not his priority—he stays focused on work and will skip it.

beats.activity: Lunch at desk: meal-prepped container + coding
beats.reasoning: Tyler eats lunch at his desk per his profile; meal-prepped on Sunday. FIFA match airs but he ignores it—work takes precedence for this workaholic.

beats.activity: Continued focused work: meetings, design docs, code submissions
beats.reasoning: Six-hour afternoon block of intensive SDE3 work; Light Rail closure (Westlake–U-District until 3pm) is irrelevant since Tyler walks to work and stays in SLU.
Tyler hits the gym at 6pm sharp per his routine; short commute from Amazon HQ to a downtown gym.
75-minute gym session; Tyler is disciplined and tracks fitness metrics. Sunny weather makes evening workout feel good.
Walk/transit back to South Lake Union apartment; allows time to cool down and hydrate post-workout.

beats.activity: Dinner, shower, personal admin: email, expense tracking, planning tomorrow's calendar
beats.reasoning: Tyler unwinds at home; tracks spreadsheets and prepares for next day. Light dinner post-gym. Winding down before bed.
8-hour 15-minute sleep window; Tyler sleeps well after intense day. Ends at 5:45am, allowing time for early wake and gym/work prep routine.

diary: Monday felt productive in that way where you don't really notice the hours passing. Got through my morning routine quick—coffee hit different today—and walked into HQ before most people arrived. Spent the morning in deep focus, which is rare these days. Grabbed lunch at my desk around 10, one of those meal-prep containers I'd prepped Sunday. The USA-Wales match was happening at 11, but honestly I barely registered it. Too locked into a tricky architectural problem we're solving. Skipped the light rail drama—would've been a mess anyway with the closure—and just headed straight to the gym after work. Hit it hard, needed to burn off something. By the time I got home, I was wrecked but in that good way. Showered, ate, handled some admin stuff I'd been putting off. Bed felt earned.
thought_process: I came into today wanting to make real progress on my SDE3 work—there's this pressure to ship solid designs, not just code—so I prioritized deep work early and protected that time. The light rail closure didn't really affect me since I'm close enough to HQ to walk; actually freed me from thinking about logistics. The World Cup match was background noise; I'm not super into soccer, and work was demanding enough that I didn't feel FOMO. Gym time was non-negotiable though—I've been stressed about some upcoming reviews, and physical activity is how I decompress. The day felt structured and intentional, maybe a little too controlled, but that's how I operate best. I'm settling into the rhythm of the week.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
